title: Cliente en inglés — preguntar idioma
input: Hi, can foreigners buy property in Dominican Republic?
reply:
Hola, claro que sí — foreigners have the exact same property rights as Dominicans here in the DR. No restrictions, no residency required.

Here's what you need to know:

You can buy, rent out, sell, and even inherit property just like a local. The whole process is protected by a real estate trust (fideicomiso) under Law 189-11 — meaning your money is held by an independent fiduciary bank from day one, not directly by the developer. That's a big deal for peace of mind.

For documentation, you'd typically need your valid passport, a secondary ID from your country, last year's tax return, bank statements from the last 3 months, and if you're salaried — a letter from your employer. We handle the KYC form (fiduciaria) after you reserve a unit.

If you're interested in financing, banks like Scotiabank and Banco Popular offer mortgages to foreigners — typically up to 70-80% of the property value.

And if you're looking at our Puerto Plata projects (Etapa 3 or Etapa 4), there's a big bonus: CONFOTUR (Law 158-01) applies, meaning you get a 15-year IPI tax exemption plus you skip the 3% transfer tax. That's a significant long-term saving. Our other projects (Crux, PR3, PR4) are not tourist-classified, so CONFOTUR doesn't apply there.

One honest heads-up: our projects don't qualify as "low-cost housing" under DGII, so the First Home Bonus (Bono Primera Vivienda) doesn't apply — but the fideicomiso protection and CONFOTUR benefits more than make up for it depending on your goals.

What are you looking for — a vacation home, a rental investment, or a place to live? That'll help me point you to the right project. 🌴
